title: Unit, Property, Mutation, Headless, GUI, and Play Tests · Program Brief: Property Tests
summary: Tests assert internal lists, fake a green result, require the developer display, and never prove a deliberate defect is detected. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the property tests increment in layered maze verification suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Generate bounded grids and assert reciprocal walls, connectivity, tree rules, route validity, cost, determinism, and state invariants.
Separate pure unit, property, mutation, integration, Tcl, Tk widget, display, accessibility, package, and player tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

A defensible solution applies Property Tests, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to layered maze verification suite.

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-property-tests — Generate bounded grids and assert reciprocal walls, connectivity, tree rules, route validity, cost, determinism, and state invariants.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-testing-properties-headless-4
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-testing-properties-headless-4.
# Case 1655: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def worked_maze_testing_properties_headless_4(model_tests: int = 96, property_cases: int = 240, killed_mutants: int = 12, surviving_mutants: int = 0, native_gates_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "gridworked4",
        "topology1655",
        "search4dekrow",
        "eventworked41655",
        "access1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if model_tests < 24 or property_cases < 64:
        return False, model_tests, "test-depth"
    if killed_mutants < 8:
        return False, killed_mutants, "mutation-coverage"
    if surviving_mutants > 0:
        return False, surviving_mutants, "undetected-defect"
    if not native_gates_named:
        return False, property_cases, "headless-overclaim"
    assert surviving_mutants == 0
    return True, killed_mutants, "layered-maze-verification"

# Evidence: maze-property-tests-jjto48
# Competency: maze-property-tests.
# Case 6240: a low-vision player session.

def evidence_maze_property_tests_jjto48(model_tests: int = 96, property_cases: int = 240, killed_mutants: int = 12, surviving_mutants: int = 0, native_gates_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_jjto48 = "jjto48-84otjj-6240"
    assert evidence_variant_jjto48
    evidence_axes_jjto48 = (
        "gridjjto48",
        "topology6240",
        "search84otjj",
        "eventjjto486240",
        "access6240jjto48",
        "recovery84otjj6240",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_jjto48)) == 6
    if model_tests < 24 or property_cases < 64:
        return False, model_tests, "test-depth"
    if killed_mutants < 8:
        return False, killed_mutants, "mutation-coverage"
    if surviving_mutants > 0:
        return False, surviving_mutants, "undetected-defect"
    if not native_gates_named:
        return False, property_cases, "headless-overclaim"
    assert surviving_mutants == 0
    return True, killed_mutants, "layered-maze-verification"

steps.why: Retrieving Property Tests through the concrete layered maze verification suite case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Property Tests model · Theory Property Tests, keep Property Tests connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Generate bounded grids and assert reciprocal walls, connectivity, tree rules, route validity, cost, determinism, and state invariants.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Property Tests model · Theory Property Tests, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Property Tests precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Property Tests model · Theory Property Tests, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 1773 during a corrupted-state recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to combine pure properties, deliberate defects, headless checks, native GUI gates, and player evidence; resource use must remain below an explicit budget; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Generate bounded grids and assert reciprocal walls, connectivity, tree rules, route validity, cost, determinism, and state invariants.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Property Tests model · Theory Property Tests, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Many random examples prove an invariant without an independent oracle.
